steps: Go to https://developers.facebook.com and log in with your Facebook account
Click 'My Apps' in the top-right, then click 'Create App'
Select 'Business' as the app type and click 'Next'
Enter an app name and contact email, then click 'Create app'
From the app dashboard, find 'Messenger' in the product list and click 'Set up'
Under 'Access Tokens', click 'Add or Remove Pages' and connect a Facebook Page
Once the Page is connected, click 'Generate token' next to the Page
Copy the Page Access Token — this is your API key (Bearer token)
To receive webhooks, configure a webhook endpoint under 'Webhooks' in the Messenger settings
To go live, switch the app from Development to Live mode

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Page Access Token
credential_fields.description: Your Facebook Page Access Token for the Messenger platform, sent as 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN'

notes: Free to use; no charges for Messenger API access
A Facebook Page is required — the token is tied to a specific Page
Long-lived page access tokens do not expire (unless the user who generated them deauthorizes the app)
App must be in Live mode and have the 'pages_messaging' permission approved for production use with non-admins
Token is passed via 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header
Webhook setup is required to receive incoming messages from users
Use the Page Access Token debugger at https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/accesstoken/ to inspect token details
